{{Infobox book
| name = Of Kings and Killers
| slug = ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers
| precedes = [[Of Killers and Kings (novel)|Of Killers and Kings]]
| follows = [[Of Darkness and Dawn (novel)|Of Darkness and Dawn]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2020
| isbn = 9780999851197
| publisher = Audible Studios on Brilliance
| pages = 401
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The novel begins with a failed assassination attempt by [[Karson]] and twenty-one patriots against [[the Emperor]], [[Calder Marten]]. Although the rebels use alchemical devices to bypass the [[Imperial Guard]], [[Calder]] survives and orders the survivors imprisoned. As [[Calder]] navigates political tensions with various [[Guild Heads]] and a potential peace treaty with the [[Regents]], he receives warnings from the prisoner [[Jerri]] about [[Shera]] of the [[Consultants]], who is prophesied to be 'the The Killer{{!}}Killer.' Meanwhile, an alchemist named [[Petal]] becomes entangled in the mysteries of the '[[Elders]]' after witnessing [[Jyrine]]’s secret studies.

[[Calder]]’s political maneuvering is complicated when the [[Navigator's Guild Head]], [[Cheska Bennett]], tasks him with recovering the lost [[Imperial]] crown to clear his massive debts. This mission coincides with a violent breakdown in peace talks at the [[Rose Tower]], where [[Regent]] [[Estyr Six]] attacks [[Calder]] after recognizing an Elder mark on his arm. The resulting battle leaves the area in ruins and claims the life of [[Baldezar Kern]]. Seeking to regain momentum, [[Calder]] investigates the disappearance of the ship *[[The Reliable]]* on a roaming island. During this expedition, his crew faces terrifying creatures like the [[Othaghor]] and suffers significant losses, including [[Andel]] being swallowed by an Elder-like beast. Amidst the chaos, [[Calder]] uses the [[Optasia]]—the [[Emperor's throne]]—to tap into a global network of [[Intent]], where he encounters the powerful being Ozriel. Ozriel reveals that the [[Great Elders]] are actually '[[Class One Fiends]]' and warns that if they possess human vessels, Ozriel will be forced to destroy the world.

As the threat of the [[Elders]] intensifies, [[Calder]] attempts to protect himself by using a body double, but he is eventually targeted by the [[Consultant assassins]]. During a chaotic battle at the [[Imperial]] Palace, [[Shera]] reveals herself as a double [[Soulbound]] and stabs [[Calder]], leaving him for dead. However, he is rescued by his allies—[[Petal]], [[Andel]], and [[Dalton Foster]]—who use alchemical elixirs and [[Soulbound]] healing to stabilize him. Amidst these struggles, [[Jerri]], acting as a secret [[Soulbound]], navigates her own dangerous path involving the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]]. 

The conflict reaches a breaking point when the [[Great Elder]] Urg’naut{{!}}Urg'naut is released, unleashing an ink-like darkness upon the Capital. [[Calder]] uses Emperor’s Intent{{!}}the Emperor's Intent to resist the void and manages to seize control of the Elder's will using Awakened chains. As the world teeters on the edge of destruction, a massive naval battle ensues against [[Kelarac]]'s army. After [[Shera]] kills [[Kelarac]], the cosmic threat remains. In a final act of desperation to save existence, [[Calder]] merges his consciousness with the echo of the former Emperor within the [[Optasia]]. This sacrifice allows them to break the threads of power holding the sky open, restoring the heavens but costing [[Calder]] and the former Emperor their individual identities. One year later, the world begins to settle into a new era of survival and mystery.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Karson]] and a group of twenty-one patriots launch an ambush against the false Emperor, [[Calder Marten]], and his [[Imperial Guard]] in the streets of the Capital. Despite using alchemical devices to bypass the [[Guards]]' enhanced senses and successfully setting [[the Emperor]]'s carriage ablaze, [[Karson]] fails to kill [[Marten]] when his musket and [[sword]] prove ineffective against the armored ruler. After [[Marten]] destroys a Shadow{{!}}shadow creature that was influencing [[Karson]]'s mind, he orders the surviving rebels to be taken to [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]].}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|While [[Calder]] observes his public coronation via a body double, he meets with various [[Guild Heads]] to discuss pursuing a peace treaty with the [[Regents]] despite distrust of their leaders. Following this meeting, [[Calder]] holds a private, highly secured audience with the prisoner [[Jerri]], who reveals that her involvement with the [[Sleepless]] was intended to facilitate direct negotiation with [[Great Elders]]. [[Jerri]] warns him that [[Shera]] of the [[Consultants]] is a primary enemy and is prophesied to be 'the The Killer{{!}}Killer.'}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|While eavesdropping on an alchemical lecture about [[Champion blood]], [[Petal]] becomes so absorbed in her notes that she loses track of time. Later, while wandering the streets, she witnesses [[Jyrine]] interacting with a group of men near what appears to be a mysterious cave that vanishes when [[Petal]] intervenes. After [[Petal]] uses alchemical tools to incapacitate two aggressive men, [[Jyrine]] reveals she is secretly studying the "[[Elders]]" and convinces [[Petal]] to keep her activities a secret.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] visits his ship, [[The Testament]], where he learns that the [[Independents]] have accepted a peace proposal and discovers that his crew members, [[Petal]] and [[Andel]], intend to leave their positions. After discussing security concerns for the upcoming summit with [[General Teach]] and [[Baldezar Kern]], [[Calder]] decides to attend the meeting in person to show strength. Finally, [[Bliss]] warns him of a secret proposal for a smaller, disarmed meeting, expressing her deep distrust regarding the influence of the [[Great Elders]].}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|After being summoned by the [[Navigator's Guild Head]], [[Cheska Bennett]], Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder [[Marten]] is offered a high-stakes mission to recover the lost [[Imperial]] crown following the disappearance of the ship *[[The Reliable]]*. [[Cheska]] promises to clear [[Calder]]'s massive debt if he successfully retrieves the relic, a task complicated by various rival factions racing to find it. Meanwhile, [[Jerri]] and a secret cabal known as the [[Sleepless]] receive divine instructions from the [[Great Ones]] to retrieve the crown at any cost.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] prepares for peace talks by choosing to wear a gold crown, despite the risk of provoking [[Regent]] [[Estyr Six]]. During the formal procession and subsequent negotiation at the [[Rose Tower]], the various [[Imperial]] and [[Independent Guilds]] meet to discuss unification and mutual defense against the advancing [[Great Elders]]. However, the meeting turns violent when [[Estyr Six]] recognizes a mark on [[Calder]]'s arm as belonging to an Elder, leading her to declare him a slave and attack the group.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|As a violent battle erupts in the [[Rose Tower]], [[Calder]] uses the Authority{{!}}authority of [[the Emperor]]'s crown to command his guards and attempt to secure weapons for his allies. The conflict spills into the streets of the [[Imperial]] Palace, where a devastating clash between [[Estyr Six]], [[Kern]], and [[Jorin Maze-walker]] leaves the area in ruins. In the aftermath, [[Teach]] miraculously survives a lethal blow from [[Jorin]], while [[Baldezar Kern]] eventually dies from his wounds.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] and his crew arrive at a roaming island to investigate the disappearance of [[Captain Tommison]] and his ship, [[The Reliable]], which has been ensnared by the jungle. Upon boarding the vessel, they discover evidence of a hurried departure or robbery and suspect the island was artificially raised to trap the ship. The mission takes a dark turn when [[Urzaia]] kills an [[Othaghor]], a twisted creature that suggests the involvement of the [[Great Elders]].}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|While [[Jarelys Teach]] undergoes a strange physical transformation due to her [[Bonereaver heart]], [[Calder]] meets with [[Cheska]] and [[Bliss]] to discuss their losing battle against the [[Independents]]. To gain an advantage, [[Calder]] uses the [[Optasia]]—the [[Emperor's throne]]—to tap into a global network of [[Intent]], seeking information on the [[Independents]] and the [[crack in the sky]]. During this dangerous process, [[Calder]] glimpses a mysterious figure standing in the void between worlds before being swallowed by the darkness.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|After narrowly escaping a giant worm creature on the beach, [[Calder]] and his crew reach a stone tower where they find the survivors of [[The Reliable]]. Although [[Captain Tommison]] initially refuses to cooperate, [[Calder]] uses the threat of seizing [[the Emperor]]'s crown to force an agreement to escape at dawn. However, [[Jerri]] discovers [[Tommison]]'s true intentions and sabotages his Alchemical munitions{{!}}alchemical munitions, causing a massive explosion that leaves the group in chaos.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|While projecting his consciousness through the [[Optasia]], [[Calder]] meets Ozriel, a powerful being who reveals that the [[Great Elders]] are actually "[[Class One Fiends]]" imprisoned in their world. Ozriel explains that if the [[Elders]] successfully possess human vessels to escape their prison, he will be forced to destroy the entire world. Upon waking, a panicked [[Calder]] attempts to send urgent messages via the [[Imperial seal]] to regional leaders and guilds, but his messengers face immediate threats from [[Elders]] and their agents.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Following an alchemical explosion that killed [[Captain Tommison]] and destroyed their munitions, [[Calder]] leads the remaining survivors on a desperate dash through the jungle toward the beach to reach their ship. During the escape, the group is ambushed by a swarm of [[Slithers]] and an amphibian-like Elder, resulting in [[Andel]] being swallowed by the creature along with the safe containing the crown. As [[Urzaia]] chases the beast into the jungle, the remaining survivors are left devastated by the loss and the chaos of the battle.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]], the [[Imperial Regent]], prepares to act as bait for the [[Consultant assassins]] by using a body double named [[Rojric]] to deliver a public address while he remains hidden in the [[Emperor's armor]]. Meanwhile, the [[Champions]] report that several of their members have been murdered by the [[Gardeners]] or [[Consultants]]. In a separate event, [[Bliss]] realizes she has been poisoned with alchemical agents and lured away from the Capital by illusions of [[Nathanael Bareius]].}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|After a harrowing encounter with [[Elderspawn]], [[Calder]] manages to secure [[the Emperor]]'s crown and rescue [[Andel]], who had been swallowed by a creature. While the crew celebrates their victory on [[The Testament]], [[Jerri]] uses her secret identity as a [[Soulbound]] to incapacitate and kill Mister Goss{{!}}Goss after he attempts to blackmail her for the crown. The chapter concludes with the crew discovering Mister Goss{{!}}Goss's body, which is attributed to the influence of the [[Elders]].}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|While delivering a speech to a hostile crowd, [[Calder]] uses an [[Awakened padlock]] to seal the [[Imperial]] Palace, trapping an assassin team inside. A chaotic battle ensues in the mist as [[Shera]] reveals herself to be a double [[Soulbound]], capable of absorbing power and bypassing even the [[Emperor's armor]]. The chapter concludes with [[Shera]] stabbing [[Calder]] and vanishing, leaving him dead on the floor as the [[Champions]] search for her.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|As the [[Lyathatan]] arrives at [[Candle Bay]], [[Calder]] reflects on the suspicious deaths of [[Tommison]] and Mister Goss{{!}}Goss, questioning if the [[Great Elder]] Ach'magut{{!}}Ach’magut is manipulating events to ensure his survival. After docking, [[Calder]] meets with [[Cheska]], the Head of the Navigators' [[Guild]], and reports that the mission failed and the crown is lost. Despite [[Cheska]]'s suspicions regarding the mysterious deaths of the other crew, [[Calder]] maintains his innocence while secretly hiding the crown on his ship.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|After the [[Champions]] bring a mortally wounded [[Calder]] to a makeshift guild house, [[Petal]] discovers he is still alive and enlists the help of [[Lotta]] to contact her allies. [[Dalton Foster]] and [[Andel]] arrive to assist, with [[Foster]] using an alchemical elixirs{{!}}elixir while [[Andel]] undergoes a risky process to become a [[Soulbound]] to provide the necessary healing power. As the team works to stabilize [[Calder]] so he can survive being removed from his protective armor, [[Calder]] experiences a disjointed vision of [[Kelarac]].}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Driven by desperation and rage, [[Dalton Foster]] uses his unique skills to create '[[Oath to Eternity]],' an Awakened pistol designed for vengeance against the brigands holding his family hostage. After the weapon kills the bandit leader [[Wells]] and his associates, [[Foster]] and his mutilated family barricade themselves in a basement to await an inevitable attack from the remaining gang. Just as the [[bandits]] breach their home, a [[Champion]] named [[Urzaia]] arrives to defeat the invaders and rescue them.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|In a dream-like projection, the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]] offers [[Calder]] a deal to share his body in exchange for global prosperity and power, but [[Calder]] rejects the offer after realizing it is a deceptive trap. Meanwhile, [[Jerri]] is interrogated by [[Meia]] of the [[Consultants]], leading [[Jerri]] to perform a dangerous summoning ritual to escape her confinement. [[Calder]] eventually wakes up in the care of his allies, discovering that they successfully rescued him and are planning to flee the Capital.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Jerri]] meets the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]], who tasks her with awakening the '[[Crawling Shadow]]' using the restored [[Heart of Nakothi]]. Meanwhile, [[Calder]] and his crew arrive at a ruined fortress to use a piece of the [[Optasia]] to amplify [[Calder]]'s powers. As they prepare, a massive, ink-like darkness consumes the horizon, signaling that Urg’naut{{!}}Urg'naut has been released.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|As the [[Great Elder]] Urg’naut{{!}}Urg'naut is released, [[Calder]] uses an [[Optasia statue]] to amplify the protective [[Intent]] of [[Hightower]] and various talismans throughout the Capital. While observing the battle, [[Calder]] witnesses [[Jorin Curse-breaker]] fighting the Shadow{{!}}shadow and sees other [[Soulbound]] like [[Shera]] and [[Meia]] engaging the threat. The chapter concludes with [[Calder]] losing himself to the overwhelming darkness of the Elder's presence, leaving his survival uncertain.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|After absorbing Emperor’s Intent{{!}}the Emperor's Intent to resist the void of [[Urg’naut]], [[Calder]] successfully holds back a devastating wave of nonexistence that erases much of the Capital. As [[Urg’naut]] breaks through the sky to battle a celestial being named Ozriel, [[Calder]] leaps from a cliff onto the [[Lyathatan]] to prevent the creature from abandoning his ship. By leveraging the Awakened chains and an alchemical sedative, [[Calder]] manages to seize control of the Elder's will.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|As the Capital descends into chaos, [[Calder]] and [[Loreli]] rescue the [[Regent]] and restore [[General Teach]] to her senses before joining a gathering of [[Imperial]] leaders in the throne room. The group, including the surviving [[Regent]] [[Estyr Six]] and a hostile [[Shera]], prepares for an imminent naval battle against the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]]. Amidst the tension, [[Calder]] secretly plans to accept a deal from [[Kelarac]], while [[Bliss]] uses a [[Consultant]]'s tip to track down and transform [[Nathanael Bareius]] into a handbag.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|As the [[Navigator fleet]] engages [[Kelarac]]'s massive Elder army, [[Calder]] and his crew fight off waves of [[Elderspawn]] on the deck of [[The Testament]]. A deceptive entity mimicking his wife, [[Jerri]], appears and attempts to force [[Calder]] into a pact with [[Kelarac]] by attacking him. After [[Calder]] kills the imposter, she is reclaimed by the sea and rises again as a possessed servant of [[Kelarac]].}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|After a brutal battle where [[Kelarac]] is killed by [[Shera]], [[Calder]] realizes the world is still facing destruction from the [[Great Elders]]. To save existence, [[Calder]] merges his consciousness with the echo of the former Emperor within the [[Optasia]] to combat the cosmic threat. Through this sacrifice, [[Calder]] and [[the Emperor]] successfully break the threads of power holding the sky open, restoring the heavens at the cost of their individual identities.}}
* '''Chapter 26:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|One year after the [[Last Battle of the Empire]], [[Andel]] and his crew prepare to depart on their own ship, Woodsman{{!}}The Woodsman, while [[Foster]] entertains children with exaggerated stories of survival. Meanwhile, in a separate location, a creature named [[Shuffles]] waits beside an unresponsive human pet. After hearing a [[Guild Head]] discuss the possibility of the human awakening, [[Shuffles]] uses a powerful word to trigger the person's consciousness.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Jerri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Shera]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Urg’naut]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Andel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Petal]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Bliss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Foster]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Emperor (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Jorin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Cheska]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Loreli]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Estyr]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Rosephus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Ozriel (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Shuffles]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Urzaia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Lyathatan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Baldezar Kern]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tyria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[General Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder Marten]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Nathanael Bareius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Meia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Reader (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Jarelys Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Bareius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Jyrine]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Jorin Curse-breaker]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Soul Collector]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kern]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Mister Goss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Dalton Foster]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Sadesthenes]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kthanikahr]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tommison]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Steward (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Cheska Bennett]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tharlos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Miss Lakiri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Silent One]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Killer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Regent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Karson]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Stonefang]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Twelve]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Jakson]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Lotta]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Capital (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Great Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Optasia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Estyr Six]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Imperial Guard]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Imperial Palace (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Regents]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Blackwatch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Independents]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Consultants]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Imperial Guards]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Sleepless]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Aion Sea]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Champions]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Navigators (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Luminian Order]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Luminian]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Champion’s Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Great Ones]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Bastion's Veil]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Empire (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[void (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Alchemist's Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Greenwardens]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Izyria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Gray Island]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Rose Tower]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Rainworth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Guild of Witnesses]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Aion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Vandenyas]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Guild of Magisters]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Alchemists]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Hightower]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Imperialist Guilds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Luminian Knights]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|roaming island}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Witnesses (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Reading]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[White Sun]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Emperor's armor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Emperor's crown (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Soulbound Vessel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Spear of Tharlos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tyrfang]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Reliable]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Sandborn Hydra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Imperial seal]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Soulbound]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Intent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Great Elder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kameira]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Ach'magut]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Emperor’s Intent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Champion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[crack in the sky]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Slithers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Awakening]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Imperial Steward]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Creeping Shadow]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Vessel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Magister]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Unknown God]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Reader burn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elder War]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elder (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Void (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL20875439W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
